summary: Holistic §1↔§3↔§5↔§7 consistency sweep across the finished PRD (3 lenses, read-only) — the final gate before freeze

model.summary: LENS A (estate-domain spine + 3-reputation model) is consistent across §1, §3, §5, and the §1.6.3 tier table. The locked model holds everywhere I checked: (1) the spine is the ESTATE rising in the house's theme, the domain expands identically at every tier (T0 estate → T1 +village/valley → T2 +region → T3 +castle-town → T4 +Edo), and only the estate spine gates tiers (pillar thresholds are bundled into the spine capstone rungs R7/V7/G7; village & origin are explicitly multipliers, never triggers — confirmed in §3.4.1, §3.6.1, §2.15d). (2) The per-tier rep arc labels match canon at EVERY tier with no stale labels: T0 stranger→friendly · T1 friendly→trusted · T2 trusted→honorary member · T3 honorary member→chief steward/yōnin · T4 MC stays yōnin/house-national — verified identical in §1.5.4 (L323-326), §1.6.3 table (L420-423), §3.4/§3.4.1, §3.6/§3.6.1, §3.7.1/§3.7.2, §5 T1.1/T2.1. (3) Village(T1) & Origin(T2) are uniformly described as one-tier optional side-tracks that never gate. (4) The only "village track / region track / climbing X society" hits are the explicit kill-disclaimers the reframes introduced (§3.4 L1877, §3.6 L1977, §3.6.1 L1943, §3.6.1 L2068, §3.7 L2117) — no residual real drift. (5) The Origin O0→O5 ladder + doubly-earned G2 gate + T2-G6/O5 Tama payoff read consistently across §1.5.3, §1.5.4, §2.15, §3.5, §3.6, §3.6.1, §3.6.2, §5 T2.1/T2.2/T2.5/T2.7. Two stale residuals survive the reframes (both contradict locked canon + the doc's own dominant wording) and one minor tier-table omission — none break the core model, hence PASS_WITH_FIXES.

findings.location: §1.12 'How §1 paces the incremental unlocks', line 725 — "...village gentle per-node meters; origin discrete milestones), and the side factions act as Influence multipliers..."
findings.issue: Stale pre-upgrade shape descriptor for the Origin track. Canon's 'Reputation systems model' UPGRADED Origin from 'discrete restored-ties milestones' to a proper one-tier rep LADDER with its own rungs (O0→O5), and every other place in the PRD reflects this (§1.5.3 L298, §2.15 L1380, §3.6.2 L2076 all say 'upgraded from discrete restored-ties milestones to a proper one-tier rep ladder'). This single line in the §1 pacing summary still calls Origin's shape 'discrete milestones' — exactly the framing the reframe was meant to kill. A reader of §1.12 alone would think Origin is still milestone-shaped, not a laddered side-track.
findings.fix: Replace 'origin discrete milestones' with the upgraded shape, e.g. 'origin a short one-tier rep ladder (O0→O5)' so §1.12 matches §1.5.3 / §2.15 / §3.6.2 and canon.

findings.location: §2.15(c) 'Rough DATA shape', line 1409 — "`FactionMultiplier { source (village|origin), influenceSpeedup, budgetShare (apportioned so the combined ≈ ½ time, never exceeded) }`."
findings.issue: Stale '≈halve' side-faction speedup encoded in the data shape. Canon locked the magnitude at ~10–15% off time-to-next-tier and explicitly says it 'supersedes the old ≈halve (patched in §1.5.4 / §1.12 / §2.15)'; §3.9 L3096 confirms '~10–15% ... supersedes the stale ≈halve'. The §2.15 PROSE was patched (it says '~10–15%' in (b) L1396 and surrounding text), but this data-shape line still says the combined speedup '≈ ½ time' — contradicting both canon and its own section. An implementer coding budgetShare from this pseudocode would build a ~50% speedup, violating the lock and making the side-tracks far more than 'felt, never a wall.'
findings.fix: Change 'apportioned so the combined ≈ ½ time, never exceeded' to reflect the locked value, e.g. 'apportioned so the combined ≈ 10–15% speedup, never exceeded'.

findings.location: §1.6.3 five-tier table, T0 row, line 419 — "**T0 — Estate** | Earn your keep and a place at the table. One declining hill estate, unlocked room by room. | ..."
findings.issue: The tier table states the per-tier standing arc parenthetically for T1 ('standing friendly→trusted'), T2 ('standing trusted→HONORARY MEMBER'), T3 ('standing honorary member → chief steward/yōnin') and T4 ('the MC stays yōnin...'), but the T0 row omits its arc label. Canon's arc is 'stranger→friendly(T0)'; the omission makes the table the only place where 4 of 5 tier rows carry the standing arc and one does not. Not a stale/wrong label — just a presentation gap in the one table the lens flags as the canonical tier reference.
findings.fix: Add the T0 standing arc to the Theme cell for parity, e.g. '(standing stranger→friendly)', matching §1.5.4 L323.

terms.summary: Lens B (names, terms, authenticity) is essentially CLEAN across all four substantive canon checks. (1) The Standing & Office pillar is 官威 (kan'i) everywhere kanji appears, in both pillar tables; the four-pillar set 武威/官威/家産/家格 → 家威 is consistent; no 政威 leftover (only documented as rejected) and no stray 官位 (the single instance is the canon-blessed homophone-disambiguation note). (2) R7 = 地方役 (jikata-yaku) / "home-field bailiff" everywhere; the lone jitō-dai mention documents it as dropped; michi-ban correctly diegetic-only. (3) T4 is framed as the DOMAIN's Edo conduit / rusui-yaku under the DAIMYŌ's sankin-kōtai "never its own" in all ~12 occurrences; zero "house's own Edo estate / lord's sankin-kōtai" leftovers. (4) All character names are consistent and macron-correct: Osen (no "Ohana"), Otsuru, Jinpachi (Kuranosuke only as "renamed from"), Munenori, Naoyuki, Genemon, Jūbei, Yagōemon, Tomita/Sōzaemon/Kageyuki/Yasubei, Akagi/Gennai, Tōkichi. The ONLY remaining issues are cosmetic macron normalizations on naturalized loanwords — no BLOCKERs, no SHOULDs touching canon substance. Notably the doc declares its own macron-lint (§6 lines 4125-4126: "flags plain-ASCII romaji that should carry a macron") yet leaves several such words plain, and is internally inconsistent on daimyo/daimyō (4 plain vs 14 macron'd). These are flagged as NITs.

findings.location: §1.1 line 133 "no real place or daimyo names"; §1.5.1 line 655 "the daimyo's confidence won"; §1.10 line 744 "no real place/daimyo/house names"; §1.10 line 749 "the house the daimyo *deputises*"
findings.issue: Internal macron inconsistency: "daimyo" appears 4× plain-ASCII but "daimyō" appears 14× with the macron (e.g. §1.7 lines 423/470, §2.16 line 1464, §3.7 line 2147, §5 lines 3762-3765). Canon (§H/§I) mandates "macrons (proper Hepburn) project-wide … no plain-ASCII slips," and the PRD's own §6 macron-lint would flag these. Same word written two ways in one document. (Note: canon's own brainstorm doc is itself inconsistent here — line 37 "daimyo" vs line 293 "daimyō" — so this is inherited, not invented.)
findings.fix: Normalize all 4 plain instances to "daimyō" to match the dominant macron'd form (proper Hepburn 大名 = daimyō).

findings.location: §6 macron-lint spec lines 4125-4126 ("the verifier flags plain-ASCII romaji that should carry a macron, so no [plain ASCII]") vs actual usage: "yokai" (e.g. lines 600/603/630/734/1148/3243/3846), "shogun/shogunal" (e.g. lines 551/667/1564/2166/3756), "ronin" (e.g. lines 1172/1916/2022/3012/4095/4373), "Osaka" (lines 466/2175/3682/3711)
findings.issue: The doc defines a macron/no-plain-ASCII-romaji lint, but several naturalized Japanese terms are left plain that strict Hepburn would macron: yokai→yōkai (妖怪), shogun/shogunal→shōgun (将軍), ronin→rōnin (浪人), Osaka→Ōsaka (大阪). These are conventionally accepted English exonyms AND canon itself writes them plainly, so they may be intentional — but as written they violate the doc's own stated lint rule.
findings.fix: Either macron these to satisfy the stated lint, OR add an explicit allow-list exception in the §6 lint spec for naturalized exonyms (shogun, ronin, yokai, daimyo, Osaka, samurai, etc.) so the rule and the prose agree. Pick one and apply consistently.

findings.issue: Low-confidence macron/name check: "Sokichi" is written plain-ASCII. If the intended reading is 惣吉/宗吉 (Sōkichi) it needs a macron; if a short-vowel reading it is fine. Cannot determine the intended kanji from the doc. Additionally it sits phonetically close to Moneylender "Tōkichi" (both end -kichi), a mild name-proximity risk in a doc that elsewhere deliberately disambiguated Osen/Otsuru.
findings.fix: Verify the intended reading: if long-vowel, write "Sōkichi"; confirm the Tōkichi/Sokichi proximity is acceptable or rename the peddler. If the short reading is intended, no change needed — flag is informational only.

xref.summary: LENS C audit of docs/prd.md against canon (2026-06-25-locked-decisions.md). The locked model holds across the load-bearing spine: the three earned-transition spines (§3.2.1/§3.4.1/§3.6.1) are mutually consistent in format and granter-escalation, and agree with the §5 T0.2/T1.2/T2.2 main-quest beats, the §1.5.1 rung tables, and the §4.8 pacing tables. The R5 de-densification (Crafting@R4, Arms-deeds-begin@R5, Arms-standing-recorded@R6, four-bar-panel@R7) is propagated everywhere with NO reversion. §7 milestones reference real systems; M2b/M3 crafting-core ownership is clean (M2b builds core, M3 surfaces the tab). Gate thresholds tie out across §3/§4/§7. Old pacing targets (~45–75 min / ~12–20 h) live ONLY as explicitly-superseded notes. All §X.Y cross-refs resolve. No contradictions found with no-management-sim, combat-earns-Arms, trade ≤⅓, or the reveal cadence. Four non-blocking issues: a wrong rung number in a cast bio (Chiyo R3 vs R4), an estate-stage E2 build-timing inconsistency (T0-R6 vs T1) between §3/§4/§7 and §5/§3.4, a stale superseded "≈ ½ time" speedup left in the §2.15 data shape (locked value is ~10–15%), and a dangling "F11" reference. None touch the 5-tier spine, the reputation-systems model, the rivals, the pillars, the pacing budget, or the Edo ceiling.

findings.location: §1.8 Cast — Estate, line 498: "**Lady Kurosawa Chiyo** | ... | Gatekeeps **houseman access (R3)** and the domestic economy"
findings.issue: Wrong rung number. The 'houseman / Trusted hand' rung is R4 everywhere else (§1.5.1 R4 'Trusted hand & houseman'; §3.2 R4 'win Lady Chiyo's regard for indoor work'; §3.2.1 explicitly makes Chiyo the R3→R4 granter; §5 T0.2 beat 6). R3 is the combat 'Yard-hand under arms' rung gated by the wolf fight + Jūbei. Calling Chiyo's houseman gate '(R3)' contradicts the earned-transition spine.
findings.fix: Change "houseman access (R3)" to "houseman access (R4)" (Chiyo grants the R3→R4 promotion into the Omoya).

findings.location: Estate stage E2 build-timing: §3.3 L1865 "| **The Workshops & Granary** | E1 | `RANK` R6"; §4.8.1 L2960 "**R6 Foreman of works** ... **2K koku** (build E2)"; §7 M3 L4584 "the E2 build fires" — vs §5 T0.4 L3195 "Buildings (E0 → E1)", §5 T1.4 L3344 "E2 — Recovering: a proper granary raised; two workshops", and §3.4 header L1900 "Estate stage span: E1 Stabilising → E2 Recovering"
findings.issue: Inconsistent on which tier raises stage E2 (granary + two workshops + low palisade, defined as E2 in §1.5.1 L262). §3.2 R6 / §3.3 / §4.8.1 R6 / §7-M3 build-or-reveal the Workshops+Granary+palisade in T0 at R6 (§3.3 even tags them stage 'E1'), while §5 T0.4 frames T0 as E0→E1 only, §5 T1.4 raises the granary/workshops in T1, and §3.4's header makes E2 a T1 span. Read literally, E2 cannot both be 'built' at T0-R6 and be the T1 stage span. The R6 log line ('the frame of a workshop is raised; the granary takes shape') and §2.17(e) ('E2 builds across T0→T1') imply E2 BEGINS at T0-R6 and COMPLETES in T1, but §4.8.1 ('build E2') and §3.3 ('E1') don't say so.
findings.fix: Make the wording consistent with E2 beginning at T0-R6 and completing in T1: relabel §3.3's 'Workshops & Granary' stage column to 'E2 (begun)'/'E1→E2', and reword §4.8.1 R6 + M3 phase 4 from 'build E2' to 'begin E2' so they no longer contradict §3.4 / §5 T1.4 completing E2 in T1.

findings.location: §2.15(c) data shape, line 1409: "`FactionMultiplier { source (village|origin), influenceSpeedup, budgetShare (apportioned so the combined ≈ ½ time, never exceeded) }`"
findings.issue: Stale superseded magnitude living as current design. The side-faction speedup is LOCKED at ~10–15% off time-to-next-tier; canon explicitly states this 'supersedes the old ≈halve (patched in §1.5.4 / §1.12 / §2.15)'. The §2.15 prose was patched (L1396–97 '~10–15%'), but this data-shape comment still encodes the rejected '≈ ½ time' (halving). Self-contradictory within §2.15 and contradicts the canon lock.
findings.fix: Replace "apportioned so the combined ≈ ½ time, never exceeded" with "apportioned so the combined ~10–15% off time-to-next-tier, never exceeded" (match L1396–97 and §4.9 item 6).

findings.location: §3.6.2 intro, line 2085: "It **opens at G2** on the **doubly-earned** gate (... **PILLAR** — travel-standing to walk the checkpointed *kaidō*; §1.5.3, F11)."
findings.issue: Dangling reference. 'F11' is cited as if it were a defined open-question/flag item, but no 'F11' is defined anywhere in the PRD (the only F-tags are the formula-fix labels F1/F2/F4 in §4.2.2). A reader cannot resolve it.
findings.fix: Drop the 'F11' token or repoint it to the real anchor for the T2-G2 double-gate (§1.5.3, or canon §'§3 ladder review resolutions' / §3.9 item 4).
